title: CI: wsl machine flakes
body:
Flakes are so bad that each PR needs like at least like 3 or more retires until the wsl task passes.
One thing we should do is to add WSL logging to see if anything shows up there, I started here https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25882 so someone should finish that up

There are like two different symptoms, total CI task timeout after 60 mins (likely should lower that again because the task time is much faster), in this case most commands just seem to timeout after 10mins.

https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5839274018340864

```
  C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image Z:\podman-machine.x86_64.wsl.tar 7d5770bf3d96
  [FAILED] Timed out after 600.000s.
  command timed out after 600.000000s: [C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image Z:\podman-machine.x86_64.wsl.tar 7d5770bf3d96]
  Expected process to exit.  It did not.
```

```
  C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine rm --force fd408f939745
  [FAILED] Timed out after 600.000s.
  command timed out after 600.000000s: [C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine rm --force fd408f939745]
  Expected process to exit.  It did not.
```

---

And then there is a "fast" fail where at some point most tasks just keep failing with WSL connection errors
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4649069861142528
https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4649069861142528/html/machine-wsl-podman-windows-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--run-basic-podman-commands-Volume-ops--1


```
C> podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image Z:\podman-machine.x86_64.wsl.tar --now ef245288215b
  Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
  The operation completed successfully. 

  Configuring system...
  Machine init complete
  Starting machine "ef245288215b"
  The operation timed out because a response was not received from the virtual machine or container. 

  Error code: Wsl/Service/CreateInstance/HCS_E_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT

  Error: the WSL bootstrap script failed: command wsl [-u root -d podman-ef245288215b /root/bootstrap] failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```

```
C> podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image Z:\podman-machine.x86_64.wsl.tar --now a2b3385349e9
  Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
  Catastrophic failure 

  Error code: Wsl/Service/RegisterDistro/E_UNEXPECTED

  Error: the WSL import of guest OS failed: command wsl [--import podman-a2b3385349e9 Z:\podman_test1271291991\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\wsldist\a2b3385349e9 Z:\podman_test1271291991\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\a2b3385349e9-amd64 --version 2] failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```

```
  C> podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image Z:\podman-machine.x86_64.wsl.tar --now fe0e4d84c6a5
  Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
  A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond. 

  Error code: Wsl/Service/RegisterDistro/0x8007274c

  Error: the WSL import of guest OS failed: command wsl [--import podman-fe0e4d84c6a5 Z:\podman_test1181544560\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\wsldist\fe0e4d84c6a5 Z:\podman_test1181544560\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\fe0e4d84c6a5-amd64 --version 2] failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```

comments.body: @craigloewen-msft any chance you might shed insight into the first failure that @Luap99 describes here ?  I believe the newer one came into play with the newer WSL recently released. Is that correct @Luap99

So in general we always had flakes with timeouts and the likes, but recently it got so worse that it affects our ability to merge PRs in a timely manner as I am force to keep pressing rerun.
We merged an VM image bump in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26341 and if I would have to bet it started to get much worse since then.  I check what we have in these images for another issue here https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/1797#issuecomment-2999721719

This is the current image:
```
WSL version: 2.5.9.0
Kernel version: 6.6.87.2-1
WSLg version: 1.0.66
MSRDC version: 1.2.6074
Direct3D version: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore version: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows version: 10.0.20348.3807
```
The older VM images had
```
WSL version: 2.4.13.0
Kernel version: 5.15.167.4-1
WSLg version: 1.0.65
MSRDC version: 1.2.5716
Direct3D version: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore version: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows version: 10.0.20348.3453
```
so my naive guess is that maybe something changed in WSL that triggers these thing at a much worse rate.

@Luap99 your guess about this being a code change in WSL seems likely to me as well. 

Are you able to take WSL logs of when you're hitting this error? And then include them on this thread or open up a new issue on the WSL repo?

Instructions for taking logs are at: aka.ms/wsllogs 

Having those logs will help us determine why this error might be happening.

Yeah about logs I need someone to fix https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25882. I don't use windows or pwershell and so cannot test this easily. Maybe @l0rd, somebody else from the team or even you can pick this up to gather the proper logs in our CI systems.

The other option is is locally keep running `.\winmake localmachine` and see if anyone can reproduce. In CI a full run takes about ~20 mins.  If look the the successful log I see 61 machine init commands so at least 61 WLS imports with starts/stops so that certainly stresses the system a fair amount I guess.
Here is our successful log for reference: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4809093531041792/html/machine-wsl-podman-windows-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html
I am having a look at #25882 this morning

@craigloewen-msft the [command to take the WSL logs](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/blob/e563689b8812154e42510a2e9a904ddafeb09ec7/diagnostics/collect-wsl-logs.ps1#L43C1-L43C124)

```
reg.exe export "HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\CLSID\{e66b0f30-e7b4-4f8c-acfd-d100c46c6278}" $folder/wslsupport-proxy.txt 2>&1 | Out-Null
```

is returning `ERROR: The system was unable to find the specified registry key or value` on our CI server (Windows Server 2022). Do you have any idea how to fix that?

@Luap99, because the Cirrus task timeout (60 minutes) is less than the Ginkgo command timeout (90 minutes), when the Cirrus timeout is reached, Ginkgo is still running, and the log retrieval is never executed. To retrieve logs in the event of timeouts, we should ensure that the Ginkgo timeout value is always less than the Cirrus task timeout.

yes, although the ginkgo timeout is unreliable (only checks in between It blocks last I checked so you need a bigger buffer to account for that)
The other thing is you can put scripts in the always block in cirrus.yml then I think it might run although maybe the timeout also cancels that not sure
Ok, got it @Luap99. https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26568, based on #25882, is ready for review.

Ok so here is a failed log where commands just timeout without an error shown:

https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4942636009652224
WSL log zip: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4942636009652224/wsl_logs/WslLogs2025-07-07_10-23-09.zip

I haven't seen one where we see WSL error codes fail yet

Another one with timeouts on podman machine rm:
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5782833148461056
https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5782833148461056/wsl_logs/WslLogs2025-07-07_18-05-50.zip

Once one timeouts everything else also hangs. Given there are no direct WSL errors visable here I am not sure if this is is a WSL error or some podman deadlock potentially?
Does anyone know how to trigger the go stacktrace on windows, i.e. on unix we just send SIGABRT (which even compiles fine on windows but of course windows doesn't have these signals so I dunno why the go lib defines them...)
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/b047cfc7e9fb5160e549fed9e16be67858c18f54/pkg/machine/e2e/config_test.go#L60-L62


Also my subjective feeling is we see much less failures now that logging is enabled. Not sure if the extra windows logging alone is enough to not cause whatever race conditions we seem to be hitting.

> [@craigloewen-msft](https://github.com/craigloewen-msft) the [command to take the WSL logs](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/blob/e563689b8812154e42510a2e9a904ddafeb09ec7/diagnostics/collect-wsl-logs.ps1#L43C1-L43C124)
> 
> ```
> reg.exe export "HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\CLSID\{e66b0f30-e7b4-4f8c-acfd-d100c46c6278}" $folder/wslsupport-proxy.txt 2>&1 | Out-Null
> ```
> 
> is returning `ERROR: The system was unable to find the specified registry key or value` on our CI server (Windows Server 2022). Do you have any idea how to fix that?

@l0rd  could you comment that line out in the downloaded script and try again?? That reg key is fine to leave out.
@craigloewen-msft thank you for your feedback. We do have [commented it out](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/cirrus/win-collect-wsl-logs-start.ps1#L70).

Another timeout one:
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4696050528681984
https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4696050528681984/wsl_logs/WslLogs2025-07-08_16-54-47.zip

---

But this one might be interesting:
```
  The operation timed out because a response was not received from the virtual machine or container. 
  Error code: Wsl/Service/CreateInstance/HCS_E_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
  Error: the WSL bootstrap script failed: command C:\Windows\system32\wsl.exe [wsl -u root -d podman-9c15505e812e /root/bootstrap] failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6166253468909568
https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6166253468909568/wsl_logs/WslLogs2025-07-08_10-14-52.zip

And afterwards tests just timeout
```
C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/basic_test.go:25
  CVE-2025-6032 regression test - HTTP
  C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/basic_test.go:291
  > Enter [BeforeEach] TOP-LEVEL - C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/machine_test.go:210 @ 07/08/25 10:39:12.369
  < Exit [BeforeEach] TOP-LEVEL - C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/machine_test.go:210 @ 07/08/25 10:39:12.37 (1ms)
  > Enter [It] CVE-2025-6032 regression test - HTTP - C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/basic_test.go:291 @ 07/08/25 10:39:12.37
  C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image docker://127.0.0.1:50940/testimage e519bfa627af
  [FAILED] Timed out after 600.000s.
  command timed out after 600.000000s: [C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image docker://127.0.0.1:50940/testimage e519bfa627af]
  Expected process to exit.  It did not.
  In [It] at: C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/config_test.go:59 @ 07/08/25 10:49:13.376
```
What is interesting about the `CVE-2025-6032 regression test` is that we pass `--image docker://127.0.0.1:50940/testimage` and that pull should fail which means the init command should not call init WSL at all to create a machine yet it hangs which means our command hangs quite early even before we try to pull. Looking at the code we perfom one check before that which is to check if a VM with the given name already exists. That seems to call `wsl -l --quiet` and then parses that output so it would be nice to know if that is were we hang.

And I repeat my question does anyone know how to trigger a go stacktrace on windows like SIGABRT does on linux?

> And I repeat my question does anyone know how to trigger a go stacktrace on windows like SIGABRT does on linux?

There is no such a signal on Windows so I don't know how and if it could be triggered.

But a standard way to generate a usermode minidump with [sysinternals procdump](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/procdump):

```pwsh
winget install -e "Microsoft.Sysinternals.Suite"

$TARGET_EXE="podman"
$DUMP_DIR="$env:TEMP\podman-dumps\"

mkdir $DUMP_DIR
procdump.exe -ma -accepteula $TARGET_EXE $DUMP_DIR
```

Once we have the dump file we should be able to open it with [WinDbg](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/crash-dump-files) (supported for amd64 golang executables, but not for arm64 yet https://github.com/golang/go/issues/57302)

> That seems to call wsl -l --quiet and then parses that output so it would be nice to know if that is were we hang.

I have built a podman executable commenting out all the instructions after `shim.VMExists()` (the function that calls `wsl -l --quiet`) and I am going to run it in loop for some time on my machine to see if it hangs here too 🤷

> > That seems to call wsl -l --quiet and then parses that output so it would be nice to know if that is were we hang.
> 
> I have built a podman executable commenting out all the instructions after `shim.VMExists()` (the function that calls `wsl -l --quiet`) and I am going to run it in loop for some time on my machine to see if it hangs here too 🤷

Worth a shot, I think the key in CI is that some prior machine init or rm command hangs or triggers a WSL failure and afterwards all the following commands hang on that ` wsl -l --quiet` call. To me that screams like a deadlock somehwere? We do some locking in podman itself but AFAICT we are using file locks and each test case runs in its own tempdir home so they should be using different lockfiles and thus another test should not be able to block following test blocks like that. So to me the more likely thing is there is a deadlock (or some other cause for a hang) in WSL itself.

@craigloewen-msft Are there any special debug commands that we could run when it hangs, we have decent control in the tests to run arbitrary commands when we detect a timeout. Would it be worth to try `wsl --shutdown` to see if that at least unblocks the following tests to avoid the follow up errors?

Anything else we can do here? This is still a problem.


Here is another log
https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5559458543173632/logs/main.log

```
  C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image Z:\podman-machine.x86_64.wsl.tar foo1
  Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
  The operation completed successfully. 
  Configuring system...
  The operation timed out because a response was not received from the virtual machine or container. 
  Error code: Wsl/Service/CreateInstance/HCS_E_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
  Error: could not create root authorized keys on guest OS: command C:\Windows\system32\wsl.exe [wsl -u root -d podman-foo1 sh -c mkdir -p /root/.ssh;cat >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys; chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys] failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```


```
  C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\cirrus-ci-build\repo\bin\windows\podman.exe machine init --disk-size 11 --image Z:\podman-machine.x86_64.wsl.tar a46a1b20d95c
  Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
  Failed to attach disk '\\?\Z:\podman_test518083807\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\wsldist\a46a1b20d95c\ext4.vhdx' to WSL2: Error: 0x80041001
  Error code: Wsl/Service/RegisterDistro/MountDisk/HCS/VM_E_INVALID_STATE
  Error: the WSL import of guest OS failed: command C:\Windows\system32\wsl.exe [wsl --import podman-a46a1b20d95c Z:\podman_test518083807\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\wsldist\a46a1b20d95c Z:\podman_test518083807\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\a46a1b20d95c-amd64 --version 2] failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```

https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5559458543173632/wsl_logs/WslLogs2025-07-24_04-41-02.zip

If you're seeing hangs in WSL, the best thing to do is collect dump logs for us and file an issue on WSL. [Instructions here](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#11-reporting-a-wsl-process-crash)!
The logs are already attached here for all the the latest errors I posted but I created https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13301

Latest image was updated too
```
WSL version: 2.5.10.0
Kernel version: 6.6.87.2-1
WSLg version: 1.0.66
MSRDC version: 1.2.6074
Direct3D version: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore version: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows version: 10.0.20348.3932
```
I keep an eye on the failure rate for now it is to early to say if it works better.
